observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the Inventory Committee to:
Conduct a complete physical count of all PPE, including local road networks, as part of the one-time cleansing initiative mandated by COA;
Prepare and submit the RPCPPE and Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network duly supported by physical inventory records within the prescribed period.
Instruct the GSO – Designate to:
Prepare the Report on Local Road Networks in accordance with COA guidelines; and
Maintain an updated Local Road Inventory, Road Map and Property Cards for all roads and their components.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to include the required disclosures on the Road Networks account in the Notes to the Financial Statements following the format provided in the Circular; and
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide a detailed description of each road project, including cost segregation of its components (e.g., pavement, shoulders, drainage, etc.) to ensure accurate classification and reporting.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all loan-related transactions and, by extension, all material cash flows are properly classified in the SCF in accordance with IPSAS 2 which includes distinguishing among Operating, Investing and Financing Activities and ensuring alignment between the SCF and the accounting records.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to:
Strictly adhere to the correct application of OCM rates and ensure that all components of the Detailed Cost Estimates are properly computed; and
Supervise the thorough staff works to be undertaken in the preparation and review of infrastructure plans and programs, especially those requiring signatures as preparers or approvers.
Instruct the BAC to:
Require the TWG – Infrastructure to thoroughly accomplish bid evaluations for all infrastructure procurements and to fully document said evaluation in the Detailed Bid Evaluation Reports of the Municipality;
Require the TWG – Infrastructure to conduct verifications, validation and ascertainment of any slippage, repairs or substandard works performed by the contractors in the submitted ongoing or completed contracts and to document said activities in the Post-Qualification Reports;
Ensure observance with procurement timelines to ensure that all procurement activities are carried in a timely manner;
Carry-out award of contracts as soon as ordinances authorizing the projects under said contracts are approved by the Municipal Mayor; and
Include issuance and downloading of bidding documents as part of the contracts submitted for COA review.
Require the BAC and the Municipal Engineer to completely indicate the preparation and approval dates of the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP), Annual Procurement Plan (APP), Program of Work, Approved Budget for the Contract, Detailed Cost Estimates, Technical Specifications, Drawings, Minutes of Pre-Procurement, Pre-Bid Conference and Opening of Bids and the TWG Report on Detailed Bid Evaluation; and
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to sign all Official Receipts issued for the purchase of bidding documents.

We recommended that Management:
Require the BAC and the Municipal Engineer to:
Cause the submission of As-Built Plans for the reviewed infrastructure projects and ensure submission of the same for other future disbursements; and
Submit the letter request for payment from the contractor on the Construction of Infanta Municipal Complex (Phase I);
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that checks are issued only after all required supporting documents such as the SWA, Inspection Report and Request for Payment, are properly completed and dated;
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to:
Issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance only after one year from the date of completion; and
Require the contractors to provide warranty security with sufficient coverage in any acceptable form prior to issuance of Certificates of Final Acceptance.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to impose on suppliers the one percent penalty on delayed delivery of goods;
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to initiate the recovery of the computed liquidated damages amounting to ₱4,050.28 from the concerned supplier; and
Instruct the GSO – Designate to strictly ensure that complete and properly dated supporting documents are submitted by suppliers before endorsing disbursement for payment.
We further recommended that Management institute internal controls to strengthen coordination among the General Services Office, BAC and Accounting Office to improve oversight, ensure strict compliance with procurement laws and regulations and safeguard the interest of the Municipality in all contractual engagements.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the BAC to require the TWG to:
Conduct and properly document all required verifications related to delivery delays, supplier performance and other technical requirements in the Post–Qualification Reports; and
Ensure that all procurement documents, including the PPMP, APP, ABC form, Technical Specifications, Minutes of procurement-related meetings and the Detailed Bid Evaluation Report, bear complete preparation and approval dates;
Instruct the Acting-Municipal Treasurer to sign all Official Receipts issued for the purchase of bidding documents to ensure accountability and authenticity.

We recommended that Management require the BAC Secretariat to:
Ensure that invitations to observers for all procurement activities conducted under the Two-Failed Biddings modality are properly included in the contract documents by preparing a comprehensive checklist of documentary requirements to be implemented during contract review and processing;
Conduct and document the BAC’s mandatory review process for failed biddings, and ensure that such documentation is submitted to the Audit Team together with the initial contract documents; and
Conduct orientation for all concerned offices on effective procurement planning strategies, with the goal of improving the success rate of procurement implementation.
We further recommended that Management require the end-users to acquire adequate knowledge of procurement procedures by incorporating budgetary provisions for capacity-building activities in their annual plans sourced from accredited institutions to ensure compliance with relevant procurement laws and to support more informed planning and decision making.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer, in his capacity as the Local Building Official, to prepare and submit an investment plan to ensure that the LBO’s 15 percent share from building permit collections is properly allocated and utilized, consistent with applicable legal and regulatory requirements and that general fund resources are preserved for other priority needs.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to furnish the quarterly and annual reports on the utilization of SEF Budget to the DepEd CO, local sanggunian and the Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG within the prescribed timelines.

Cash donations recorded as Other Payables
Cash donations received by the LGU from ERAMEN Minerals, Inc., amounting to ₱1,211,400.00 was treated as a liability and recorded in the books of accounts as Other Payables instead of Grants and Donation in Cash which is not in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS I, Section 112 of PD No. 1445 and the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; hence, understating Grants and Donations in Cash account and Scholarship Grants/Expenses accounts by ₱1,211,400.00 and ₱1,200,000.00, respectively, and overstating the Other Payables account by ₱11,400.00.
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS I provides that:
Financial statements shall present fairly the financial position, financial performance and cash flows of an entity. Fair presentation requires the faithful representation of the effects of transactions, other events and conditions in accordance with the definitions and recognition criteria for assets, liabilities, revenue and expenses set out in IPSASs.
In addition, Section 112 of PD No. 1445 provides that “Each government agency shall record its financial transactions and operations conformably with generally accepted accounting principles and in accordance with pertinent laws and regulations.”
Furthermore, COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 prescribes the Revised Chart of Accounts in the recording and reporting of the financial transactions of LGUs which defines the Grants and Donations in Cash as grants and donations in cash received from private sector or international institutions without specific purpose.
ERAMEN Minerals Inc., is a corporation engaged in mining activities and has selected the LGU as the beneficiary of the former’s CSR Program.
Relative thereto, the LGU received cash donations from ERAMEN Minerals, Inc. totaling ₱1,211,400.00 for CY 2021 relative to the implementation of the CSR Program, details are as follows:
However, review of the collection vouchers and JEVs disclosed the foregoing receipts of cash donations totaling ₱1,211,400.00 were recorded as Other Payables instead of Grants and Donations in Cash which is not in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS I and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; hence, the Grants and Donations in Cash and Scholarship Grants/Expenses accounts were understated by ₱1,211,400.00 and ₱1,200,000.00, respectively, and overstated Other Payables account by ₱11,400.00.
Based on the executed MOA, ERAMEN Minerals Inc. had given the Municipality the responsibility to submit project proposal relative to implementation of the CSR Program. It could imply that ERAMEN Minerals Inc. is giving the authority to the Municipality to decide on what PPAs is to be implemented under the said CSR Program based on the needs of the Municipality to be addressed; hence the grant from ERAMEN Minerals, Inc. should be considered as a donation without specific purpose.
The understatement of the balance of Scholarship Grants/Expenses, and Grants and Donations in Cash accounts and the overstatement of Other Payables account in such material amount affected the fair presentation of the said accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entries to correct the errors in the recording of the receipt of donations from ERAMEN Minerals Inc., and the utilization thereof to present fairly the balances of Grants and Donations in Cash, Scholarship Grants/Expenses and Other Payables accounts;
The following is the proposed journal entry:
Scholarship Grants/Expenses		1,200,000.00
Other Payables				     11,400.00
Grants and Donations in Cash                     1,211,400.00
During the exit conference and through his letter-reply, the Municipal Accountant explained that he recorded the receipt of cash donations from ERAMEN Mineral, Inc. since the latter opposed the recording of said cash donation as donations in the books of the LGU. The Municipal Accountant also informed the Audit Team that according to ERAMEN Mineral, Inc., the LGU will just serve as an intermediary for its CSR Program, however, during the exit conference, the Audit Team insisted that the said cash donations should be recorded in the books of accounts since based on the MOA, ERAMEN Minerals Inc. had given the LGU the authority where to use the said cash donations depending on the needs of its constituents. Nevertheless, the Municipal Accountant through his letter-reply had expressed his willingness to comply with the audit recommendations. He also informed the Audit Team that he already effected the necessary adjusting entries to record the remaining balance of the cash donations amounting to ₱11,400.00 recorded as Other Payables as income in the books of accounts.
Misclassification of assets and expenses
Expenses and assets arising from various transactions of the LGU amounting to ₱3,287,491.16 and ₱191,900.00, respectively, were not taken-up in the books of accounts using the appropriate accounts in accordance with Section 112 of PD No. 1445 and Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 which is not in conformity with Section 112 of PD No. 1445; hence, resulted in misclassification of accounts presented in the financial statements.
Section 112 of PD No. 1445 provides that each government agency shall record its financial transactions and operations conformably with generally accepted accounting principles and in accordance with pertinent laws and regulations.
In addition, COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 prescribes the Revised Chart of Accounts in the recording and reporting of the financial transactions of LGUs.
Audit of DVs and JEVs for CY 2021 disclosed that various transactions of the LGU were not recorded in the books of accounts using appropriate accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts. Summary of the said transactions are as follows:
The recording of the foregoing transactions using inappropriate accounts resulted in the misclassification of expense and asset accounts presented in the Statement of Financial Performance and Financial Position, respectively.
The Municipal Accountant explained that he only followed the account code used in the preparation of the budget relative to the foregoing transactions.
The Audit Team would like to emphasize that it is the responsibility of the Municipal Accountant to analyze and properly record every transaction to its appropriate account classification in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts to provide Management and other users of financial statements accurate and more reliable information.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and OIC-MBO to review thoroughly the transactions before recording in the books of accounts to ensure proper and accurate recording of transactions and assigning of account code in the preparation of annual and supplemental budgets, respectively, in order to avoid conflicts between the accounting records and RAAOs. We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and OIC – MBO to review the Revised Chart of Accounts for uniform use of accounts.
The Municipal Accountant and OIC-MBO were amenable to the audit recommendations. They have committed to thoroughly review the transactions and will ensure that proper accounts will be used in the recording of transactions and assigning of account code in the preparation of annual and supplemental budgets.

We recommended that Management:

Stated in his letter-reply dated April 11, 2022, the OIC-MBO admitted that they had erroneously granted the CNA for FY 2021 based on BC No. 2020-5 dated November 4, 2020 which provides the guidelines on the grant of CNA Incentive for FY 2020 instead of BC No. 2021-03 dated November 17, 2021 which provides the guidelines on the grant of CNA Incentive for FY 2021. The OIC-MBO admitted that they were not aware that the available balances of appropriation for Advertisement, Publication and Printing, and Subscription Expenses considered as an allowable MOOE where the CNA Incentive can be sourced based on BC No. 2020-5 were already not considered as allowable MOOE in BC No. 2021-03; hence, resulted to the grant of CNA in excess of the savings from the allowable MOOE for CY 2021.
The OIC-MBO also said in his letter-reply that although there are no definite systems improvement approaches provided in the approved CNA, the LGU is continuously implementing various systems improvement such as the establishment of Policies and Guidelines in the Use of Cellular Phone and Communication/Telephone Expenses, Establishment of Policies on the Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles and the faithful compliance of the LGU to the guidelines of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of supplies which resulted to the generation of savings as evidenced by Appropriation Ordinance No. 4 s. 2021. Nevertheless, the OIC-MBO had expressed his commitment to adhere to the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for the RPT Receivable and Deferred RPT totaling P2,846,591.70 and the variance of P15,863.89 in order to reflect their correct balances and to fairly present them in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjusting entries thru Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 2022-01-001 dated January 03, 2022 and submitted a copy to the Audit Team via electronic mail.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to cause the immediate titling of the parcel of land located at Barangay Gumian with land area of 10,000 square meters in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The application for the title of this parcel of land was made on December 11, 2020, with follow-up on status of application on May 31, 2022. The Municipality is now waiting for the issuance of the Original Certificate of Title (OCT).

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor: (1) instruct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to include all inventories of the Municipality in the RPCI and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation with the accounting records; and (2) require the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of supplies/inventories initially as an asset account and recognize the expense once the inventories are consumed/distributed for proper matching of asset and expense in accordance with IPSAS 12.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO conducted physical count of inventories during the year, and the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO commented that both agreed to (1) conduct the physical count of inventories, (2) reconcile and update their records and (3) resolve the huge variance and make necessary adjustment to the records of both offices. Further, the GSO will also submit Monthly Report of Supplies Issued to the Accounting Office. Moreover, the GSO committed to resubmit the RPCI with indicated values of the counted inventories and that the foregoing reports to be made will have indicated unit values for proper reconciliation of records.
observations.reason: No reconciliation was made. The RPCI includes items with no indicated unit values of the counted inventories, thus making it more difficult to reconcile both records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in tracing the records in order to provide the adequate information on the balances of receivables reflected in the schedule and to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the account for the fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still tracing the records.
observations.reason: Ongoing verification and correction of the negative and unreconciled balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) exert effort in tracing the records in order to provide the adequate information on the balances reflected in the relative schedules and to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of these accounts for their fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1; (b) exert effort in tracing information on the age and validity of the claims for Due to Officers and Employees account in the amount of P7,087,649.03; and (c) request from the Sangguniang Bayan the authority to revert the total balance of P5,852,408.03 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future and in compliance with the requirement of Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of tracing the records and verifying the records and the amount of P5,745,630.21 was reverted to the General Fund through JEV No. 2022-05-001946 dated May 30, 2022, while the difference between the reverted amount and balance for reversion recommended was charged to different subsidiary accounts, were closed/offset to the corresponding subsidiary accounts.
observations.reason: On process of verification of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) exert effort in tracing from the records the information on the age and validity of the claims for Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable; and (b) request from the Sangguniang Bayan the authority to revert the total balance of P171,625.74 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future and in compliance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 for the fair presentation of the payable account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to trace the details of Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable. The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments thru JEV No. 2022-05-000070 dated May 30, 2022 and submitted a copy to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 by completing the inventory of insurable properties and expediting the gathering of all the supporting documents required by the GSIS for the immediate processing of their application for property insurance and providing the necessary funding thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Management through the MGSO commented that they allotted P1,000,000.00 through Supplemental Budget No. 1 for application of property insurance with the GSIS in 2021. However, due to various needs of the LGU, the funds were augmented to other accounts. This year, the MGSO has reapplied for appropriate property insurance of all uninsured properties of the Municipality and will immediately process the payment upon receipt of the assessment from the GSIS.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation no. 5, Page Nos. 35 to 37, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the receipt and utilization of the BGCM in the Special Account under the General Fund pursuant to DBM LBC No. 125.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the proceeds from sale of bid documents under Trust Liabilities account in the General Fund; 

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger for Trust Liabilities account specifically for the proceeds from the sale of bidding documents; and 

c. Direct the BAC to refrain from using the collections from the sale of bidding documents aside from payment of honoraria and overtime pay as authorized in the foregoing BC.

We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to stop the practice of directly charging the payment of overtime services to the unobligated appropriation for Salaries and Wages – Regular pertaining to the vacant positions without an Appropriation Ordinance by providing a Supplemental Budget; and 

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to record the payment of overtime services as Overtime and Night Pay in accordance with the Chart of Accounts in order to present the correct balances of the expense accounts.

We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the OIC - Municipal Budget Officer to avoid direct charging of the monetization of leave credits as well as other expenses to the balances of appropriations in the Annual Budget and use the savings to augment the deficient item of appropriation duly supported with SB Resolution or ordinance; and 

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the payment of monetization of leave credits as Other Personnel Benefit account to present the correct balances of the expense accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Social Welfare Development Office (MSWDO) to submit the copy of valid ID, Barangay Certification with specimen signature and Social Amelioration Card of the identified beneficiaries to the Audit Team for further evaluation; and 

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with MSWDO, to submit the following lacking documents to the Audit Team to ascertain the veracity of receipt of financial subsidy of eligible beneficiaries:

Identification Documents of recipients with signatures indicated therein; and

Authorization letter from the payee and identification document of the person who received the assistance and signed the payroll other than the qualified payee.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging non-priority expenses to SEF and accommodating regular expenditures for activities for which DepEd has direct mandate to implement; and 

b. Require the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to review the annual budget of the Local School Board (LSB) for CY 2021 and make necessary realignment through LSB Resolution for those appropriations which are allocated in the SEF budget not consistent with the LSB Budget.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to send letter-query to GPPB whether posting of procurement data for all contracts awarded through emergency procurement under Bayanihan Act can still be made.

We also recommended that Management require the BAC to secure updates and new issuances of the GPPB or any other agencies related to government procurement to ensure strict and timely compliance therewith.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat to review and discuss the GPPB Resolution, and to initiate the assessment of 2020 procurement performance of the LGU using the APCPI system and recommend to the HoPE for approval and submission to the GPPB.

We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the OIC-HRMO to submit to the Municipal Accountant the approved DTRs of the public health workers which shall be the basis in the computation of subsistence and laundry allowance; and 

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to compute the subsistence and laundry allowance based on the DTRs submitted and not on a monthly fixed-rate basis.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to demand OR (Accountable Form No. 51) from the PNP upon release of their share from the collection of traffic violation fees as proof of payment.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to demand OR from other LGUs and NGAs before the issuance of check in the grant of financial assistance to ensure that government funds are properly accounted for and utilized for public purpose; and 

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to demand from the Punong Barangay who received the financial assistance to submit the OR of expenses relative to the Cultural Celebration of the Barangay.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to request a copy of SB Resolution to support the grant of allowances including the specific rate of allowance to National Government Officials.

We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person to prepare the annual status reports on the institutional mechanisms for gender mainstreaming and submit the same to the DILG Regional Office for consolidation.

We also recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to include all M&E activities in the GPB.

submit immediately to the Audit Team the required Financial Statements and
supporting schedules/documents for CY 2020 within five calendar days from
receipt of the Audit Observation Memorandum. Henceforth, ensure that the
succeeding reports are submitted within the prescribed period pursuant to existing
rules and regulations.

General Services Officer (MGSO) to (a) expedite the preparation of all the necessary

on the balances of payables reflected in the schedules and to make the
necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of these accounts
for their fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with

present the balances of the accounts in the financial statements in accordance with

statements.

However, the Office Supplies Inventory remained unreconciled and the report on physical count of Accountable Forms was not included in the submitted RPCI. The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the audit recommendation to record purchases of supplies/inventories initially as asset and recognize the expense upon consumption/distribution.
observations.reason: The concerned departments are in the process of determining the balances of inventories on hand and those utilized/consumed for the proper reporting of assets and expenses. The Municipal Accountant committed to record purchases of supplies/inventories initially as asset and recognize the expense upon consumption/distribution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to stop the setting up of liability account for the share in the IRA collections for 20% Development Fund using the Due to Special Accounts, take up the necessary adjusting entries to ensure fair presentation of the affected accounts required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the existing regulations by completing the inventory of insurable properties and expediting the gathering of all the supporting documents required by the GSIS for the immediate processing of their application for property insurance and providing the necessary funding thereof.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO submitted the list of insurable properties of the Municipality to GSIS for their review and approval and is waiting for the amount of premium to be paid based on their assessment.
observations.reason: The Slaughterhouse and its content insurable value has not been established due to pending appraisal of the Appraisal Committee and the MGSO is still waiting for the Deed of Sale from the supplier for the Hog Van. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 9, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide the details and supporting documents for the balances and forward the same to the Municipal Treasurer; and require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the total amount of P177,653.03 to the respective National Agencies to avoid the incurrence of penalties and charges.
observations.managementAction: Analysis and verification of the balances are now in progress by the Municipal Accountant. Due to PhilHealth and Due to Pag-IBIG were remitted to the appropriate National Agency within the prescribed period. The balances of Due to GSIS were not yet established due to lack of details and supporting documents.
observations.reason: Balances of Due to PhilHealth and Due to Pag-IBIG were remitted to the appropriate National Agency. The Due to BIR account balance was not remitted leaving an unremitted balance of P84,098.98. The account Due to GSIS still has unestablished balance due to lack of details and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to stop charging the operating and administrative expenses of the MDRRMO to the LDRRM Fund; and require the MDRRMO to properly identify projects and activities that are to be charged to LDRRMF as enumerated under Item 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Budget Officer stopped charging the operating and administrative expenses of the MDRRMO to the LDRRM Fund and increased the budget for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) of the MDRRMO in 2020 and 2021 amounting to P170,000.00 and P324,648.00, respectively. The MDRRMO properly identified projects and activities to be charged to LDRRMF pursuant to existing guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to ensure that the Commission’s approval of the 10-year SWMP will be submitted to the National Solid Waste Management Commission and any additional information that may be required by the approving agency will be facilitated in order to acquire its approval before the year end.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year SWMP was submitted and updated and was approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission under NSWMC Resolution No. 1391 Series of 2020 on October 30, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the reconciliation of the accounts and remit whatever balance due the agencies to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and to enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail the benefits from the said offices.
observations.managementAction: The unremitted balances were traced; remittances in CY 2019 are not intact. Investigation ongoing.
observations.reason: Remittances in CY 2019 are not intact; unremitted balances not provided with details and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the General Services Officer-Designate to exert diligent effort
and exhaust all the means to reconcile the Report on the Physical Count with the
accounting records to ensure that the PPE account presented in the financial
statements are fairly presented and free from material errors and
misstatements.

We also recommended that Management require the General Services
Officer-Designate and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards and
Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC), respectively, for each
class of property, plant and equipment listing therein the data as to its
acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, depreciation, disposal and other
information about the asset, for proper accounting and monitoring of the PPE
account and to facilitate identification thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the
accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management
System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and
COA Circular No. 2015-002 be strictly complied. Further, we recommended the
Management to require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary
records and to provide depreciation costs for roads and road components for
every road network and to instruct the General Services Officer to maintain a
Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network
Property Cards for all roads and its components.

We also recommended the Management to create an inventory committee
to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
the Road Network System to be reconciled with the accounting records.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that
Management exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership
over the lots through Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of
Municipality.

Also, Management coordinate with the municipal officials concerned in
the documentation and processing of documents, which, if accomplished, is vital
in the determination of the proper valuation or appraised value of the acquired
land.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the
Management direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct thorough review, with
the involvement of other accounting personnel, and analyze the details of
receivables to ascertain their correctness, validity and collectability and exert
extra effort to collect the same which includes sending demand letters to persons
who have known address to facilitate collections. Those can no longer be
accounted or settled may be requested for write-off to clear the accounts subject
to the requirements and procedures in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-
005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended the Management to exert diligent effort to collect the
loans receivable. The specific address and the whereabouts of the recipients
should be identified and demand letters should be sent to the individuals
concerned to enforce collections thereof.

We reiterated the prior year’s recommendation to dispose the
unserviceable properties of the agency in accordance with the procedures and
requirements provided in Sections 76 and 79 of PD 1445 to generate additional
funds for the agency’s operations and prevent the unserviceable properties from
further deterioration and loss of its value.

We recommended the Management the following courses of action:
a.) Ensure that all of its insurable properties are adequately covered by the
General Insurance Fund (GIF) administered by the Government Service
Insurance System (GSIS) in compliance with Republic Act No. 656 or the
“Property Insurance Law”, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated
July 13, 1973, in order to avoid the risk of non-indemnification in case of
damage or loss due to fortuitous events.
b.) Submit a complete inventory of insurable properties to GSIS for them to
make appropriate evaluation whether the items included can be covered or
not, annual report of which must also be submitted to the audit team.

We recommended the Management the following courses of action:
(a) Refrain from assigning Job Order Workers who are not properly
designated and bonded to perform collection functions;
(b) Recall if possible, employee/s holding a permanent position in the
Municipal Treasury Office who are detailed in other offices to
discharge collection functions as part of the duties and responsibilities
inherent to their positions;
(c) Review plantilla position for possible addition of personnel to perform
collection functions and other duties in the Treasury Office; and
d) Ensure strict adherence to existing rules and regulations for the
collection of revenues and receipts to properly safeguard government
funds.

We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Treasurer
to regularly update her cashbook at the end of the day and make the required
certification every month-end as to its correctness and completeness in
accordance with the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to submit the monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable
Forms to the Audit Team within the reglementary period provided in COA
Circular 92-382.

We recommended that the Management require the MDRRMO to
ensure strict compliance with the guidelines provided in COA Circular No
2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and JMC No. 2013-01 of NDRRMC-DILG-
DBM and other DRRM related rules and regulations particularly in the
preparation of the LDRRMFIP of the agency to establish proper management
of the fund and the regularity of all charges therefrom.

We also recommended that the Management require the MDRRMO to
submit to the Audit Team a revised LDRRMFIP and a list of projects/activities
for CY 2019 which were charged to the unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund
in order for the Audit Team to evaluate properly the charges made by the LGU
to the said 5% calamity fund.

We recommended that the local government unit transfer all
unexpended/unobligated balances of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE from the General Fund to the
Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund” in the Trust Fund books.

We also recommended that Management require the Municipal
Treasurer to maintain separate bank account for the special trust fund account
for the unexpended balance to avoid the usage of the fund for purposes other
than financing the disaster risk reduction and management activities.

We recommended the Management to evaluate strictly the need for
consultants taking into consideration their expertise, training or education and
whose skills are not possessed by the regular employees of the LGU to avoid
unnecessary expenses that could be used to other significant projects, programs
and activities of the Municipal Government.

We also recommended that Management require the Consultant on
Barangay Affairs to submit accomplishment reports to support his claim for his
service rendered in accordance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary
adjustments in the books of accounts to record the 15% and 5%
shares of the OBO and DPWH, respectively, from the collection of
building permit fees for the years 2017 to 2019 pursuant to PD
1096.
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor the amount to
be automatically retained with the Municipality to be used for
OBO’s necessary operating expenses and the amount to be
remitted to BTr representing the 5% share of DPWH; and
c. Require the Municipal Engineering Office as the OBO to prepare
Program of Work detailing the manner on the utilization of the
15% share from the collection of building permit fees, and
properly observe the guidelines in the preparation and submission
of the program of works to the DPWH Secretary thru the
NBCDO.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Human
Resource Management Officer to require all employees who are rendering
overtime services to use the biometric system for their Time In and Out in
order to achieve the intended purpose of the system. The machine generated
DTR’s shall form part of the supporting documents for overtime pay.

We recommended the Management to instruct the Supply Officer/GSO
Designate to conduct pre-repair and post-repair inspections of motor vehicle as
part of management sound internal control. Also, accomplish and submit pre-
repair and post-repair inspection reports to support the payments thereof.

We recommended that the Local School Board maximize the utilization
of Special Education Fund for the priority programs/projects/activities set forth
under the Local Government Code and the aforesaid Joint Circular to attain its
desired objectives.

We recommended the Management to require the current Municipal
School Board to reorganize the composition of the MSB and ensure to include
the SK Federation President as one of the Members for proper representation
of the youth and include only those explicitly specified as members of MSB in
compliance with Section 98 (3) of Section 98 (3) of RA 7160.

We reiterated the prior year’s recommendation that the Accounting
Office, in cooperation with the Treasury Office, ensure the complete submission
of accounts within the prescribed period for timely verification and audit of
accounts pursuant to Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 of the Rules and Regulations on
the Settlement of Accounts, and Section 39 (1) and (3) of P.D 1445 to avoid
disciplinary actions and/or penalties provided for under Section 39 (3) of P.D
1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
1. Require the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and
submit monthly Statement of Appropriations, Allotments,
Obligations and Balances not later than the twentieth day after
the end of each month;
2. Instruct the OIC-Budget Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant
to perform quarterly reconciliation of the budget and actual
amounts in order to prepare periodically the Statement of
Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts; and
3. Ensure that the year-end set of financial statements is submitted
on or before the 14th day of February of the ensuing year.

We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to strictly adhere to the existing rules and regulations in the
recording of transactions to provide users of financial statements more reliable
information.

We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board to submit immediately the revised Solid Waste
Management Plan to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for its
approval in accordance with Section 17 of Ecological Solid Waste Management
Act of 2000 and implement it accordingly.

We recommended the Management to direct the GAD Focal Person to
use the GAD Plan and Budget in the preparation and submission of GAD
Accomplishment Report together with all the supporting GAD-related reports
in conformity with the provisions set forth in JMC No. 2013-01 to facilitate the
proper evaluation of the Municipality’s performance in addressing gender
issues and concerns.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Services
Officer to prepare all the necessary documents, ensure to complete the appraisal of all
unserviceable properties and cause its immediate disposal in order to avoid further
deterioration and possible loss, and to generate additional income from its disposal.
Thereafter, require the completion of the reconciliation of the PPE accounts, investigate
and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to:
• cause the immediate titling of the parcel of land located at Barangay Gumian
with land area of 10,000 square meters in the name of the Municipality; and
• exert extra effort to locate the documents relative to the parcel of land acquired
on January 6, 1990 in order to establish the ownership and avoid possible third-
party claim.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to complete the reconciliation of the cash accounts and adjust any
discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct balances for the fair
presentation of the cash accounts in the financial statements in accordance with
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to expedite the completion of
RPCI in order to facilitate the reconciliation of inventory accounts and conduct
physical count of the other inventories of the Municipality; and
• require the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of
supplies/inventories initially as an asset account and recognize the expense
account once the inventories are consumed/ distributed for proper matching of
asset and expense accounts in accordance with IPSAS 12.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
stop the setting up of liability account for the share in the IRA collections for 20%
Development Fund using the Due to Special Accounts account, instead the
reciprocal account Due to Other Funds and take up the necessary adjusting entries
to ensure fair presentation of the affected accounts required under Paragraph 27 of
IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services
Officer to ensure that all the remaining insurable properties of the Municipality are
covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the existing
regulations by expediting the gathering of all the supporting documents required by the
GSIS for the immediate processing of their application for property insurance and
providing the necessary funding thereof.

We recommended that Management:
i. Require the General Services Officer-Designate to:
Prioritize the conduct of a periodic physical count of its inventories and prepare the report using the prescribed Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to be submitted to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of NGAS for LGUs. Where necessary, create an inventory committee to expedite such inventory-taking; Maintain Stock Cards for each specific type of Inventories; and Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued. The SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS shall be submitted to the Chief Accountant.
ii. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to: Strictly apply the perpetual inventory method in recording inventory purchases and issuance thereof pursuant to Section 114, Volume I of NGAS Manual for Local Government Units. In addition, maintain detailed inventory records for each inventory item; and Prepare JEV on the basis of SSMI and designate an accounting personnel with sense of responsibility to maintain an updated Supplies Ledger Cards for each specific type of Inventories.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive provide a stockroom or space for the use of General Service Office to keep the supplies and materials purchased including equipment which are to be issued to the requisitioning head of department needing the same.

We recommended that management instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to existing rules and regulations in the recording of purchases of drugs and medicines and medical supplies except for those purchased out of the petty cash fund in accordance with PPSAS Chart of Accounts and maintain perpetual inventory records; and
General Services Officer-Designate to maintain stock cards to account for the receipt and disposition of procured medicines in coordination with the Municipal Health Office for the actual number/quantities of medicines and supplies issued for a particular period and with the LDRRM Officer on the actual number of medicines and supplies distributed.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the misclassification of accounts in the recording of equipment and furniture and fixtures for proper presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommended that Management ensure proper recording of every financial transactions in conformity with the PPSAS Chart of accounts. Further, we recommended Management to strictly comply with the revised documentary requirement for government transactions to ensure complete documentation in payment of all claims against government funds.

We recommended that management require the MDRRM Officer to prepare annually the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 which should contain the following information:
a. Functional Classification
b. List of Programs/Projects/Activities
c. Implementing Office
d. Schedule of Implementation
e. Expected Output
f. Funding Source
g. Amount of Appropriation
The LDRRMFIP shall also include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and provide a detailed presentation of every project and activities that will be charged thereof.

We recommended that management instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) ensure proper planning and programming of disaster related projects/activities in order that available DRRM funds can be utilized calamity-related programs, projects and activities of the Municipality.
Likewise, we recommended that management submit justification/s that the expenses incurred for the expansion of the economic enterprise (water system) of the Municipality is related to the expenses enumerated in above-cited JMC No. 2013-1 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG and necessary as one of the conditions given by the DBM RO I, and submit a copy of LDRRM Plan incorporating therein the proposed projects.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Officials concerned to fully account all distributions of goods/supplies/materials to end-users and submit to the Audit Team the distribution list with complete details as to specific item/s and quantity duly received and signed by the beneficiaries.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the PPSAS Chart of Accounts in the recording of financial transactions to present the proper asset and expense accounts in the financial statements and avoid inaccurate and misleading information.
Further, we recommended that adjustments be made on the Welfare Goods for Distribution account duly supported with documents, if necessary, to reflect the correct amount in the financial statement.

We recommended that Local School Board consider the allocation of ECCD Programs in the SEF Budget pursuant to Section 7(b) of RA No. 10410 and observe that said allocations should be within the allowed areas/items as provided in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.1, s. 2017 in order to address the needs of young children as well as to enhance and sustain the capabilities of service providers and the welfare of the community.

We recommended that Management pay their obligations only after delivery of the goods or items purchased not only in compliance with the aforecited provisions of laws but also to avoid the risk of non-delivery of goods or non-performance of obligations by the supplier and issuance of notice of suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the guidelines set forth in Section 82 of Local Treasury Operations Manual in the preparation of disbursement voucher to ensure effective and efficient management in disbursements and establish the reliability of disbursement records.
We also recommended that Management require the approving Officers to indicate the date when the DVs are signed and ensure that all paid disbursement vouchers and the supporting documents are stamped “PAID” in accordance with Section 83 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual to avoid the possible re-use of disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (1) review thoroughly the claims for honoraria of the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG to ensure correctness of claims and payment made in conformity with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular 2004-5A; and (2) monitor the prescribed ceiling for the payment of honoraria and ensure that no payments are made in excess thereof to avoid issuance of notice of disallowance in audit.
We also recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to verify the payments made and require all the personnel concerned for the immediate refund of overpayment of honoraria received by them.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere to the provision of Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 and shall prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the GPPB within (fourteen) 14 calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant should strictly comply with the provisions stated in the GAA pertaining to net take home pay of regular employees to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support for their daily needs until the next payday.

We recommended that Municipality continue to appropriate one percent of its Internal Revenue Allotment for the LCPC programs and activities and to fully maximize the funds allocated for the implementation of the programs, projects and activities that would address the prevention of juvenile delinquency and welfare of the children. Also, consider the preparation of local codes, plans and children’s profile as listed in DILG Memorandum Circular that can possibly help the LCPC in its mandate to help and support the children in the locality.
Likewise, include in the LCPC program the funding for applicable training and capacity building activities for service providers to enhance their abilities and competence.
Further, provide the Audit Team with the list of recipients of school supplies per child development centers to ascertain the propriety and validity of the transaction.

We recommended that management:
 Prioritize the creation/establishment of sanitary landfill in line with the criteria set forth in the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act to ensure the protection of public health and environment; and
 Instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the lot intended for Sanitary landfill to properly establish the LGU ownership over the said lot and so as not to impede the creation of the required sanitary landfill.

We recommended that management direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the GAD AR together with all the supporting GAD-related reports in conformity with the provisions set forth in JMC No. 2013-01 to facilitate the evaluation of the Municipality’s performance in addressing gender issues and concerns.

We recommended that management meticulously identify the PPAs to effectively address gender issues before inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget and ascertain that all expenditures for GAD-related activities are efficiently and effectively in line with the GAD objective and activities identified in the GPB.
Likewise, we also recommended the management to require the GAD Focal Person to submit justification on the observations noted above regarding the efficiency and effectiveness of the agency’s GAD Activity in addressing gender issues raised.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to expedite the completion of the reconciliation of the property, plant and equipment account in order to establish the correct PPE balances, and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted. Moreover, submit to the Audit Team the IIRUP duly supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the GSO are still in the process of reconciling the PPE of the Municipality. The GSO submitted the complete IIRUP as of year-end.
observations.reason: Due to lack of documents to support the properties acquired long time ago. The appraisal value of the PPEs listed in the submitted IIRUP are not yet provided. Appraisal Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to look into the Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the Registry of Deeds, if any and/or the supporting tax declaration of the various lots and cause the immediate titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Two out of the four parcels of land were already registered in the name of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Due to lack of documents and absence of approved survey plan that is a requirement for titling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to trace the causes of the non-reconciliation and make the appropriate adjustments for the fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already identified the cause of the non-reconciliation.
observations.reason: Due to the practice of the Municipal Accountant to record the share of the 20% Development Fund from the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: • the Municipal Treasurer to submit the certified list of taxpayers showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the Accounting Office. • the Municipal Accountant to record the beginning balances of SET Receivable and Deferred SET and to make the appropriate adjustment, if any. Henceforth, follow the accounting procedure for Special Education Tax stated under Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The certified list of taxpayers showing their name and the amount due and collectible for the year were submitted to the Accounting Office. The Municipal Accountant is now following the accounting procedure for Special Education Tax.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to expedite the completion of RPCI in order to facilitate the reconciliation of inventory accounts and conduct physical count of the other inventories of the Municipality; and • require the Municipal Accountant to fully comply with the guidelines set forth in PPSAS 12 for the proper recognition of inventories and expenses.
observations.managementAction: Some of the lacking information in the RPCI were provided by the MGSO. There are purchases delivered directly to end-user monitored by the MGSO. The Municipal Accountant is still recording purchases delivered directly to end-user as expense upon delivery.
observations.reason: There are still important information that will be needed in the reconciliation. There are still purchases delivered directly to end-user not monitored by the MGSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to immediately submit their liquidation reports to the Accounting Office, and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year and to ensure that liquidation of cash advance is accurately recorded. Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Accountable Officers submitted their liquidation reports. The Municipal Accountant monitored all the cash advances at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the liquidation of Petty Cash totalling P59,800.00; • consider the creation of Assistant Accountant with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan and providing the necessary budget therefor; and • instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the reconciliation of the cash accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated the Petty Cash. Considered creation of Assistant Accountant during the year but instead, hired an additional job order with background in accounting. Cashbooks outdated and lack of manpower for the reconciliation of cash accounts.
observations.reason: Not Implemented: creation of Assistant Accountant not pursued; outdated cashbooks and lack of manpower for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to monitor and evaluate the progress of the SALINTUBIG Project to avoid further delay in the implementation and to ensure that preliminary requirements such as Detailed Engineering Design (DED) are considered.
observations.managementAction: Projects was completed. Majority of the targeted projects for the year were implemented/on-going implementation in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the Office of the Human Resource Management before the approval of loan to ensure that the employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the mandated minimum allowed take home pay of P5,000.00 as provided under Section 48 of the GAA for FY 2018.
observations.managementAction: The ILGEA loan process was monitored and the mandatory monthly net take home pay provision in the GAA was observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer to ensure that all the remaining insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS by expediting the gathering of all the supporting documents required by the GSIS for the immediate processing of their application for property insurance and providing the necessary funding thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the Municipal General Services Officer to insure all the remaining insurable properties mentioned above. The GSO is on-going tracing of documents needed for the application of property insurance for the Municipal Slaughterhouse.
observations.reason: There are still three municipal properties not covered with appropriate property insurance; on-going tracing of documents; absence of some required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the timely approval of the annual appropriation ordinance in compliance with Section 319 of the Local Government Code of 1991 to ensure that targeted projects/programs/activities under the 20% Development Fund are attained.
observations.managementAction: The budget for CY 2019 was approved on November 12, 2018. Majority of the targeted projects for the year were implemented or on-going implementation in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) and the Sangguniang Bayan for the provision of additional budget for the operating and administrative expenses of the MDRRM Office and require the GSO to submit to the Audit Team the report of all inventory accounts for the MDRRM Office.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO conducted physical inventory of supplies and relief goods for stockpiling as of December 31, 2019 and included in the RPCI submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Due to the retirement of the MBO, additional budget for the operating and administrative expenses was not provided in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MENRO to update the WACS required by the EMB and secure the approval of the 10-year SWMP from the National Solid Waste Management Commission.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO complied with the necessary information required by the EMB in their WACS. The updated SWMP was submitted to EMB-DENR in February 2020.
observations.reason: The revised 10-year plan was submitted only in February 2020 for approval of EMB-DENR; additional data requested by EMB-DENR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality submit
explanation on the incurrence of operating and
administrative expenses of P684,466.74 utilizing the
LDRRMF and account for the procured supplies and
relief goods totaling P336,189.64. Also, we
recommended the full utilization of the LDRRMF
through the implementation of various disaster
prevention and mitigation projects that will address
disaster prevention and mitigation to strengthen and
develop the LGU’s preparedness to natural and man-
made hazards and disasters in accordance with existing
law, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor commented that they will
coordinate with the Municipal Budget for additional
budget in the General Fund to sustain the operating and
administrative costs of the MDRRMO. Also, they
committed to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason:
