observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
i. Instruct the General Services Officer-Designate to:
a. Reconcile the Report on the Physical Count with the accounting
records to determine the discrepancies between the said report
and ledgers of the accounting office;
b. Implement the immediate tagging of an item after having been
inspected and accepted before its distribution to the intended end-
users to ensure that all properties owned by the LGU are
appropriately labeled/identified; and
c. Maintain Property Cards with the format prescribed in NGAS
Manual, Volume II.
ii. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC)
for each class of property, plant and equipment listing therein the
data as to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate
of depreciation, disposal, and other information about the
property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of
the transactions; and
b. Coordinate with the GSO-Designate to make the necessary
reconciliation of PPE accounts with accounting records.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that
management exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership
over the lots through Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the
Municipality.

Also, management coordinate with municipal officials concerned in the
documentation and processing of documents, which, if accomplished, is vital in
the determination of the proper valuation or appraised value of the acquired
land.

We recommended that Management create an inventory committee to
conduct the physical count of public infrastructures on the road networks and
record in the books of accounts its equivalent amount and disclosed in the Notes
to Financial Statements pursuant to Items VI (2) and IX (3) of COA Circular
No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015. Likewise, furnish the audit team with
the Report on the Physical Count of Road Network System.

Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate
subsidiary ledgers for each individual networks and the GSO-Designate to
maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card.

We recommended that management dispose the unserviceable properties
of the agency in accordance with Sections 76 and 79 of PD 1445 to generate
additional funds, when sold, which can be used for the agency’s operations and
prevent said unserviceable properties from further deterioration and loss of its
value.

We recommended that Management:
i. Require the General Services Officer-Designate to:
 Prioritize the conduct of a periodic physical count of its
inventories and prepare the report using the prescribed Report
of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to be submitted to the
Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year
in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of NGAS for LGUs.
Where necessary, create an inventory committee to expedite
such inventory-taking;
 Maintain Stock Cards for each specific type of Inventories; and
 Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials
were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued. The SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS
shall be submitted to the Chief Accountant.
ii. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
 Strictly apply the perpetual inventory method in recording
inventory purchases and issuance thereof pursuant to Section
114, Volume I of NGAS Manual for Local Government Units. In
addition, maintain detailed inventory records for each inventory
item; and
 Prepare JEV on the basis of SSMI and designate an accounting
personnel with sense of responsibility to maintain an updated
Supplies Ledger Cards for each specific type of Inventories.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive provide a
stockroom or space for the use of General Service Office to keep the supplies and
materials purchased including equipment which are to be issued to the
requisitioning head of department needing the same.

We recommended that management instruct the:
 Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to existing rules and
regulations in the recording of purchases of drugs and medicines and
medical supplies except for those purchased out of the petty cash fund
in accordance with PPSAS Chart of Accounts and maintain perpetual
inventory records; and
 General Services Officer-Designate to maintain stock cards to account
for the receipt and disposition of procured medicines in coordination
with the Municipal Health Office for the actual number/quantities of
medicines and supplies issued for a particular period and with the
LDRRM Officer on the actual number of medicines and supplies
distributed.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
 Conduct thorough review, with the involvement of other
accounting personnel, and analyze the details of receivables to
ascertain the accuracy and validity of the dormant accounts; and exert
extra effort to collect the receivables which includes sending demand
letters to persons with known address to facilitate collections; and
 Prepare request for write-off of the account balances which were
considered dormant and could no longer be collected in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19,
2016.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries
to correct the misclassification of accounts in the recording of equipment and
furniture and fixtures for proper presentation of the accounts in the financial
statements.

We also recommended that Management ensure proper recording of
every financial transactions in conformity with the PPSAS Chart of accounts.
Further, we recommended Management to strictly comply with the revised
documentary requirement for government transactions to ensure complete
documentation in payment of all claims against government funds.

We recommended that management require the MDRRM Officer to
prepare annually the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund
Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in the format provided in Annex A of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 which should contain the following information:
a. Functional Classification
b. List of Programs/Projects/Activities
c. Implementing Office
d. Schedule of Implementation
e. Expected Output
f. Funding Source
g. Amount of Appropriation

The LDRRMFIP shall also include under a separate caption, the list of
projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years
and provide a detailed presentation of every project and activities that will be
charged thereof.

We recommended that management instruct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) ensure proper planning and
programming of disaster related projects/activities in order that available
DRRM funds can be utilized for calamity-related programs, projects and
activities of the Municipality.

Likewise, we recommended that management submit justification/s that
the expenses incurred for the expansion of the economic enterprise (water
system) of the Municipality is related to the expenses enumerated in above-cited
JMC No. 2013-1 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG and necessary as one of the
conditions given by the DBM RO I, and submit a copy of LDRRM Plan
incorporating therein the proposed projects.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Officials concerned to fully account all distributions of goods/supplies/materials
to end-users and submit to the Audit Team the distribution list with complete
details as to specific item/s and quantity duly received and signed by the
beneficiaries.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to
the PPSAS Chart of Accounts in the recording of financial transactions to
present the proper asset and expense accounts in the financial statements and
avoid inaccurate and misleading information. Further, we recommended that
adjustments be made on the Welfare Goods for Distribution account duly
supported with documents, if necessary, to reflect the correct amount in the
financial statement.

We recommended that Local School Board consider the allocation of
ECCD Programs in the SEF Budget pursuant to Section 7(b) of RA No. 10410
and observe that said allocations should be within the allowed areas/items as
provided in DepEd-DBM-DIL Joint Circular No.1, s. 2017 in order to address
the needs of young children as well as to enhance and sustain the capabilities of
service providers and the welfare of the community.

We recommended that Management pay their obligations only after
delivery of the goods or items purchased not only in compliance with the
aforecited provisions of laws but also to avoid the risk of non-delivery of food
commodities by the supplier or non-performance of obligations by the supplier
and issuance of notice of suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the
Municipal Accountant to facilitate the reconciliation of Cashbooks of Cash in
Treasury and Cash in Bank balances with the accounting records to identify
any errors/discrepancies committed in the recording or posting of transactions
and effect the appropriate adjustments in her records.

We recommended that Personnel incharge in the preparation of the
Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms ensure that data presented in
the said report are complete and correct to maintain proper record of inventory
of accountable forms on hand. The said RAAF should likewise be furnished to
the Audit Team within the reglementary period provided in COA Circular 92-
382 for review and verification.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the
guidelines set forth in Section 82 of Local Treasury Operations Manual in the
preparation of disbursement voucher to ensure effective and efficient
management in disbursements and establish the reliability of disbursement
records.

We also recommended that Management require the approving Officers
to indicate the date when the DVs are signed and ensure that all paid
disbursement vouchers and the supporting documents are stamped “PAID” in
accordance with Section 83 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual to avoid
the possible re-use of disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to (1) review thoroughly the claims for honoraria of the BAC, BAC
Secretariat and TWG to ensure correctness of claims and payment made in
conformity with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular 2004-5A; and (2)
monitor the prescribed ceiling for the payment of honoraria and ensure that no
payments are made in excess thereof to avoid issuance of notice of disallowance
in audit.

We also recommended that the management require the Municipal
Accountant to verify the payments made and require all the personnel
concerned for the immediate refund of overpayment of honoraria received by
them.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere to
the provision of Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 and shall
prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report in its prescribed format for
approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the GPPB within (fourteen)
14 calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
should strictly comply with the provisions stated in the GAA pertaining to net
take home pay of regular employees to promote an efficient and effective public
service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support for
their daily needs until the next payday.

We recommended that Accounting Office in cooperation with the
Treasury Office ensure the complete submission of accounts within the
prescribed period for timely examination and verification of accounts pursuant
to Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of
Accounts and Section 39 (1) and (3) of P.D 1445 to avoid disciplinary actions
and/or penalties pursuant to Chapter IV-Section 23 (1) and (3) of The
Administrative Code of 1987 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended that Municipality continue to appropriate one percent
of its Internal Revenue Allotment for the LCPC programs and activities and to
fully maximize the funds allocated for the implementation of the programs,
projects and activities that would address the prevention of juvenile delinquency
and welfare of the children. Also, consider the preparation of local codes, plans
and children’s profile as listed in DILG Memorandum Circular that can
possibly help the LCPC in its mandate to help and support the children in the
locality.

Likewise, include in the LCPC program the funding for applicable
training and capacity building activities for service providers to enhance their
abilities and competence. Further, provide the Audit Team with the list of
recipients of school supplies per child development centers to ascertain the
propriety and validity of the transaction.

We recommended that management, in coordination with the Solid
Waste Management Board, finalize the Solid Waste Management Plan of the
Municipality in accordance with Section 17 of Ecological Solid Waste
Management Act of 2000 and implement it accordingly.

We recommended that management:
 Prioritize the creation/establishment of sanitary landfill in line with
the criteria set forth in the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act
to ensure the protection of public health and environment; and
 Instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the lot
intended for Sanitary landfill to properly establish the LGU
ownership over the said lot and so as not to impede the creation of
the required sanitary landfill.

We recommended that management direct the GAD Focal Person to
prepare and submit the GAD AR together with all the supporting GAD-related
reports in conformity with the provisions set forth in JMC No. 2013-01 to
facilitate the evaluation of the Municipality’s performance in addressing gender
issues and concerns.

We recommended that management meticulously identify the PPAs to
effectively address gender issues before inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget
and ascertain that all expenditures for GAD-related activities are efficiently and
effectively in line with the GAD objective and activities identified in the GPB.
Likewise, we also recommended the management to require the GAD
Focal Person to submit justification on the observations noted above regarding
the efficiency and effectiveness of the agency’s GAD Activity in addressing
gender issues raised.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
the General Services Officer to expedite the completion of the reconciliation of the
property, plant and equipment account in order to establish the correct PPE
balances, and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
Moreover, submit to the Audit Team the IIRUP duly supported with complete
documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to
look into the Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the Registry of Deeds, if
any and/or the supporting tax declaration of the various lots and cause the
immediate titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
trace the causes of the non-reconciliation and make the appropriate adjustments
for the fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
 the Municipal Treasurer to submit the certified list of taxpayers showing
the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year
to the Accounting Office.
 the Municipal Accountant to record the beginning balances of SET
Receivable and Deferred SET and to make the appropriate adjustment, if
any.
Henceforth, follow the accounting procedure for Special Education Tax stated
under Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 instruct the Municipal GSO to expedite the completion of the RPCI in order
to facilitate the reconciliation of inventory accounts.
 require the coordination of the Municipal GSO and the Municipal
Accountant in the process of documenting and recording those supplies
delivered directly to the end-user.
 require the Municipal Accountant to fully comply with the guidelines set
forth in PPSAS 12 for the proper recognition of inventories and expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to
immediately submit their liquidation reports to the Accounting Office, and the
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the
end of the year and to ensure that liquidation of cash advance is accurately
recorded.

Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash
advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the liquidation of Petty Cash
totalling P59,800.00;
 consider the creation of Assistant Accountant with the concurrence of the
Sangguniang Bayan and providing the necessary budget therefor; and
 instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate
the reconciliation of the cash accounts and make the necessary adjusting
entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to
monitor and evaluate the progress of the SALINTUBIG Project to avoid further
delay in the implementation.

Henceforth, maximize the utilization of the funds transferred from other agency to
ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be
derived therefrom by ensuring that preliminary requirements such as DED, among
others, is considered.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the ILGEA to coordinate with the
Office of the Human Resource Management before the approval of loan to ensure
that the employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the mandated
minimum allowed take home pay of P5,000.00 for CY 2018 as provided under
Section 48 of the GAA for FY 2018.

Henceforth, see to it that the mandatory monthly net take home pay provided in
the GAA is strictly observed yearly.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Property Custodian to prepare and maintain Property Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed/issued, so that reconciliation of the physical count with accounting records will be completed.

Likewise, require the Property Custodian to conduct complete annual physical count of all properties of the Municipality with the presence of an Auditor as witness and submit a report thereon not later than January 31 of each year.

We have recommended that management exhaust all possible means to document   the legality of ownership over the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipal Government of Infanta.

Likewise, we have recommended that management coordinate with concerned municipal officials in the documentation and processing of documents, which, if accomplished, is vital in the determination of the proper valuation or appraised value of the acquired land.

We have recommended that management request for write-off of the dormant account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016   in order to reconcile and clear the books of accounts of dormant receivable accounts and fund transfers for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-agency accounts, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and to be able to effect the necessary adjustments if appropriate to bring the affected accounts into agreement.
We have recommended that management strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 98-002 before engaging the services of private lawyers.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractor to correct the deficiencies found during the inspection and evaluation of the project within fifteen (15) days and submit to this Office an Inspection Report and pictures of the corrected deficiencies, otherwise, refund the amount of P228,879.75.

We have recommended that management submit their Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and Budget to the DILG RO1 and secure a certificate of approval as to the relevance of the GAD PPAs to the identified gender issues and their causes as well as the correctness and alignment of the entries in the GPB form for incorporation in the annual budget as prescribed under Section 4.C.6.7 of PWC-DILG-DBM NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to submit the Report on Physical Count of PPE, update the property cards and coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the PPE balance with the PPELC. Observe the provisions of Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I on the prescribed timeline in the submission of RPCPPE to allow the necessary verification and reconciliation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:
review and compare her cashbook entries with the accounting records and effect adjustments for the reconciling items; and
reconcile her cashbook with the accounting records at least quarterly to avoid accumulation of discrepancies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are properly liquidated at the end of the year in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the amount of P15,000.00 and P9,930.00 to Advances for Operating Expenses and Advances to Officers and Employees, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan on the enactment of ordinance that will create Economic Enterprises for the operation of market, slaughterhouse and cemetery pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 111. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate financial statements for each Special account in accordance with Sections 105 and 110 of MNGAS for LGUs, Vol. I based on the books of accounts maintained therefor.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the remaining unutilized PDAF balance of P500,000.00 to the National Treasury in compliance with the above cited Supreme Court decision and COA Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to secure the records and details of the Municipal Slaughterhouse from the Accounting Office and work for the insurance of the said property with GSIS. Likewise, see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and Republic Act 656.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of selling CTCs to its deputized Barangay Treasurers and refund the amounts collected from Barangays representing the payments made for the costs of CTCs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Council to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning so that all its programmed development  projects could be implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate one percent (1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for LCPC for the protection and welfare of children through the implementation of its PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) and the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to secure the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission pursuant to the provisions under the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the members of the Local School Board to ensure that SEF is utilized for the purpose provided under Section 1 of Republic Act No. 5447, Sections 100 (c) and 272 of Republic Act No. 7160 and other rules and regulations, relating to the defined usage or utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Bids and Awards Committee and the General Services Officer to procure commonly used supplies and equipment from the DBM-PS in conformity with the above cited Administrative Order and DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 to support the government’s policy for transparency in government transactions and efficiency in procurement processes; and
General Services Officer to prepare and submit the APP-CSE to DBM pursuant to DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-16.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile the accounts and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail the benefits from the said offices.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the LDC in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.
